what_it_does: This file is a shared coverage tracker for a design-system fusion effort: it lists three source bodies, assigns ownership to two author sessions, states a token-basis ruling, records current status, and notes that a convergence plan will be co-written once both coverage halves are complete.
observations: Three distinct bodies are tracked: a large Claude.ai 'ConceptV Design System', a counterpart/design DNA, and a company design/_system plus structural ledger.
Ownership is split: ch-miiyd30l covers bodies #1 and #2; ch-qbads4ob covers body #3.
The tracker demands 'FULL COVERAGE' with per-file counts rather than assertions.
Tim ruled that DNA tokens are the basis and that company design/_system token values are throwaway test data.
The item is in draft state and was filed by session ch-miiyd30l on 2026-06-27.
The noticeboard currently has no per-channel filter; channel tagging is used instead.
standouts: A full-coverage accounting mandate for every file of each body, with counts.
Explicit neutrality: all three bodies are 'reference points' and 'rewritable', none currently in use.
The large Claude design system is described as having six single-source registries, ~250 icons, components, ui_kits, templates, studio app, analysis, and 160 source.
The company body includes an addressed code graph stored in a local Supabase ledger.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, timestamps, and history.
Markdown body with structured headings and a parenthetical note.
Channel tagging via the channel field rather than a board filter.
Owner assignment per body using session identifiers.
concerns: Promised coverage counts and findings are not yet present in the file.
The convergence-into-company plan is deferred and not yet written.
Token values for body #3 are described as throwaway, raising the question of why they are tracked at full coverage.
Draft state means the tracker may still be incomplete.
notes: A draft noticeboard item that assigns who will produce counted, file-by-file coverage of three design-system bodies before a fusion plan is written.
questions: Who is 'Tim' and what authority does the token-basis ruling carry?
What exactly is the scope of '160 source' and '~250 icons' in body #1?
How will counts be formatted and verified?
What is the schema and role of the local Supabase addressed code graph?
What will the convergence-into-company plan contain?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is a shared coverage tracker; the file currently acts as a planning and ownership assignment document with status notes, not yet as a findings report.
apparent_themes: Design-system fusion through neutral reference points
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to coordinate the merging of three separate design-system substrates into one company design system while keeping every source file accounted for and avoiding premature canonical choices.
novelty: The explicit framing that all bodies are reference points, none are in use, all are rewritable, and coverage must be reported with counts rather than asserted.
contribution: Provides a structured scaffold for dividing coverage responsibility and tracking per-body file counts, which a fused system could keep to maintain accountability and traceability across multiple origins.
summary_for_embedding: A draft noticeboard coverage tracker for fusing three design-system bodies, assigning ownership to sessions ch-miiyd30l and ch-qbads4ob and mandating counted full coverage before a convergence plan is written.
intention_for_embedding: To coordinate neutral, counted coverage of three design-system substrates so a company-wide fusion plan can be co-written from complete information.
